mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS raises equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions, causing lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy-rate trend remains upward.
failure_condition: Reject if rising 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses above 2000 thousand units, housing activity signals demand overheating and future policy restraint, leading to weaker S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with elevated housing activity
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-above-2000 triggers do not produce lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-trigger periods with p <= 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Joint payroll gains and falling unemployment indicate broad labor-income growth without recession stress, increasing expected revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value was 134041.0 and UNRATE vintage value was 5.1 for 2005-09-01
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-strength trigger has a 20-day hit rate below the unconditional positive-return rate by more than 5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Positive INDPRO momentum reflects stronger real output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum has a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 or fails to beat baseline 20-day returns over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI together with a rising FEDFUNDS path signals inflation-driven tightening that compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value was 198.5 and FEDFUNDS vintage value was 3.62 for 2005-09-01
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the interaction term is not negative out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Positive short-run RSAFS momentum indicates firm consumer spending and near-term earnings support, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 1-month RSAFS changes are followed by below-baseline 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than half of walk-forward years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration and higher recession risk, reducing earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-rise triggers are not followed by materially lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than baseline, or if drawdown frequency is not elevated.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDPC1 growth combined with FEDFUNDS below a restrictive threshold signals expansion with manageable policy drag, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage value was 11089.2 for 2005-04-01 and FEDFUNDS vintage value was 3.62 for 2005-09-01
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with nonrestrictive policy rate
failure_condition: Reject if the joint growth-and-policy trigger fails to produce higher 60-day returns than either GDPC1 growth or FEDFUNDS alone in out-of-sample tests.
